title: Chain-Road Dawn
note: Generated from Wilderland liberation Ch1 spine (canonical with story-spine.json).

frames.title: Cage Line
frames.body: Mist sits on the Chain-Road like damp wool soaked in livestock math. Iron cages rattle on ox-wains while overseers count numbers instead of names — and spit when the count comes up short. You are Nine-Mark: thrall, fighter, and cargo that punches back when the whip gets lazy.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Flask Sermon
frames.body: Road dust writes temporary freemarks on every boot. Memory of cages arrives uninvited, then leaves when named. Ash does not quit, and Quill notices, drinks, and almost approves.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Yard Gossip
frames.body: Powder grit under your nail smells like unfinished court. You practice stance until rage becomes usable instead of loud. Freedom keeps charging rent — pay it in miles.

frames.title: Road Vermin Tax
frames.body: Three road vermin block a ford with a rope and a grin. Their leader — missing two fingers and all shame — offers “protection” for coin, a story, or a bruise. Quill’s hand rests near his bolt like punctuation.

choices.label: Pay the damn rope
choices.approach: Buy the ford; save steel for Vern.

choices.label: Scare them honest
choices.approach: Show the freemark and the crossbow’s opinion.

choices.label: Trade a Chain-Road story
choices.approach: Give them a tale sharp enough to sell twice.

frames.title: Quill's Ugly Joke
frames.body: At dusk Quill asks whether you want to be a freeman who hunts, or a hunter who happens to be free — then drinks like the answer might offend him. The difference, he says, is who you apologize to when the smoke clears. “And don’t you damn quit either way.”

choices.label: Freeman who hunts
choices.approach: Keep the moral order: freedom first, warrants second.

choices.label: Hunter who is free
choices.approach: Admit the work shapes the man.

choices.label: Neither — Lyra’s man
choices.approach: Refuse Quill’s categories; keep the vow central.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Seal Weather
frames.body: Heat makes the freemark itch like unfinished business. Your wrist freemark itches whenever eyes linger too long. Quill packs the silence like powder — dry, useful, dangerous.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Powder Prayer
frames.body: A child’s chalk freemark on a fence post has already been scrubbed. Quill says, “Pretty country. Ugly ownership. Pass the damn bottle.” Collar mud still clings; freemark ink is drying on purpose.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Stubborn Mile
frames.body: Marshal Halbrecht Quill lights a match off a warrant nail, drinks, and looks almost human. Road vermin sell water and buy stories about numbered men who would not quit. Ash sets the pace like a man owed a name and too stubborn to renegotiate.

frames.title: The Binder Arrives
frames.body: A lean marshal in travel-stained grey tips a ruined hat to the overseer like a man haggling over mule feed and mildly disappointing weather. Halbrecht Quill — freehold marshal, warrant binder, one scarred eye that never quite closes on a lie — pays coin for your papers without looking away. “Pretty muscle,” he drawls, whiskey still on the breath. “Ugly price. I’ll take the stubborn one.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ring Memory
frames.body: Wind carries iron smell farther than any honest voice. Somewhere east, ink still invents people into inventory. Continue — soft roads make soft collars again.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: East Debt
frames.body: Overseer laughter carries farther than any hymn on this road. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. Paper in your coat weighs less than iron and more than sleep.

frames.title: Quill Names the Work
frames.body: “I buy thralls who can ruin the men who sold them,” Quill says, not unkind, flask catching firelight. “If that offends your poetry, walk. If it fits your teeth, load — and if you quit mid-road, I’ll leave you for the crows with a receipt.” Ash loads.

frames.title: Vern's Joke
frames.body: A traveler repeats Vern’s favorite joke about unpaid tribute and burned kitchens. Nobody laughs. Quill tips the man a copper for the geography hidden in the cruelty, then mutters that hell of a comedian still makes hell of a warrant.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. You check the freemark and find it still yours — for now. The Wilderland watches and keeps its receipts.

frames.title: Road Wargs Ambush
frames.body: Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps ever will. A hanged poster of your face lies about the eyes — always the eyes. You walk because the alternative still smells like rust — and quit is not a word you own. Then road wargs force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Road Hygiene
frames.body: A warrant nail still bleeds pine sap where paper used to hang. Ash rolls a shoulder that still remembers iron’s manners. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

frames.title: Paper Freedom
frames.body: Quill slides a stamped sheet across the wagon board and uncorks a dented flask like punctuation. Freedom on parchment, conditional as a hangman’s courtesy: hunt the guilty he names, keep steel sharp, ask no soft questions. “Say yes,” he offers, almost kindly, “or climb back into inventory. I don’t do poetry, and I don’t ride with quitters.”

choices.label: Take the warrant path
choices.approach: Accept conditional freedom; work for Quill.

choices.label: Bargain for a name first
choices.approach: Ask whose name this work is really for.

choices.label: Test the man's honesty
choices.approach: Watch for a twitch that says he buys thralls for worse work.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: Dust lifts off the track in pale sheets that lie for a living. A freemark widow sells directions and warnings in the same sentence. Chain-Road shrinks behind you like a bad religion losing believers.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: Cage wheels still squeak in the memory of your spine. Ash keeps counsel; Quill keeps a ledger of silences and a flask of worse truths. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

frames.title: Nine-Mark Burial
frames.body: Quill has you scratch Nine-Mark into roadside dirt and kick it apart. “Numbers are cages that fit in a mouth,” he says. “Answer to Ash, freemark, Lyra — never inventory. And don’t you quit back into a digit.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
frames.body: Somebody buried a whip and marked the dirt with hope. Quill mutters law under his breath like a protective curse, then swears for emphasis. You spit iron taste and keep the freemark facing out.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
frames.body: A hanged scarecrow wears Cade’s check like a joke that landed. Ash learns freeman posture: chin up, exits counted, no bowing to seals. Dust settles on everything except the vow.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Night Watch
frames.body: A roadside shrine wears three names and one empty nail. The road offers three bad choices and one worse delay. Somewhere a cage door practices its last slam.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Iron Humor
frames.body: Quill’s ruined hat casts a verdict-shaped shadow. Quill’s crossbow speaks better law than half the magistrates east — and swears better too. Quill tips his ruined hat at unfinished justice and takes a pull.

frames.title: Collar Off
frames.body: The overseer pries the collar with a hooked key that smells of rust, old sweat, and other people’s surrender. Skin burns where iron sat for seasons. Quill tosses the ring into roadside mud like trash that once owned a man — then grinds it under his heel, just to be thorough.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
frames.body: Your shadow arrives first and looks less free than you feel. A cage-tender spit misses your boots by manners, not mercy. The road keeps its receipts in blood and dust alike.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
frames.body: Two riders tip hats too friendly for the hour. Ash counts exits without moving his mouth — old habit, good habit. You walk west, then south, then wherever vengeance keeps maps — stubborn as weather.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Hard Mercy
frames.body: Quill’s hat brim cuts the sun into a usable blade; the scarred eye does the rest. You rehearse Lyra’s name until it stops sounding like a bruise. Road vermin scatter; serious hunters do not.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Vermin Bargain
frames.body: Crows argue over something dead you hope is not destiny. The Wilderland watches and declines to explain itself. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

frames.title: Road Wargs Ambush
frames.body: The collar scar itches when ox-wains pass — muscle memory of inventory. The next mile pretends to be empty and fails the audition. Somewhere a collar yard waits to learn a different inventory: revolt. Then road wargs force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
frames.body: Distant cane fields clap like soft applause for bad systems. Vern’s ledger scrap burns colder than any campfire comfort. Quill files Lowhedge under “paid in blood or nearly” and takes a pull.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Cane Wind
frames.body: A dead mule teaches economics better than any clerk. You practice not answering to a number when strangers call. You walk on because stopping is how numbers return.

frames.title: First Mile Free
frames.body: Boots that never chose a direction now choose west, then south, at Quill’s nudge. Crows watch the cages dwindle and look personally offended. Freedom feels like hunger with better manners and a loaded crossbow for a chaperone.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Binder Math
frames.body: Nine-Mark is a name you are actively murdering with each mile. A joke dies mid-laugh when Cade’s check appears on a sleeve. Steel rests light; resolve does not.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Flask Sermon
frames.body: The Wilderland files today’s silence under “pending violence.” Ash refuses to romanticize the road; the road returns the favor. Freedom keeps charging rent — pay it in miles.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Yard Gossip
frames.body: Quill counts cartridges the way priests count sins — carefully, then drinks anyway. You check the freemark and find it still yours — for now. Paper freedom first; real freedom files later — Quill’s oldest sermon.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Seal Weather
frames.body: Night insects keep score of every secret step you still take. Quill drawls that quitters make excellent fertilizer and worse freemen. You spit iron taste and keep the freemark facing out.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Powder Prayer
frames.body: Wagon ruts braid into a grammar only hunters read fluently. Somewhere a horn asks a question steel usually answers. Chain-Road shrinks behind you like a bad religion losing believers.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Stubborn Mile
frames.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. Your wrist freemark itches whenever eyes linger too long. Lyra’s sale date ticks quieter than a clock and louder than fear.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ring Memory
frames.body: Quill’s ruined hat casts a verdict-shaped shadow. Rations taste like postponement, pepper, and old promises. Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

frames.title: Orc Chainers
frames.body: Three chain-orcs peel from the pine edge, still smelling of the cages they sold this morning. They want their property returned with interest. Quill calmly loads a crossbow and drawls, “Show them the paperwork is yours — and that the warranty expired.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: East Debt
frames.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Dust becomes a third companion with poor hygiene and worse jokes. The road keeps its receipts in blood and dust alike.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: A freeman laughs too loud, then checks who heard him. You trade a story for water and keep the ending private. The Wilderland watches and keeps its receipts.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quiet Counsel
frames.body: Quill buys your freedom like a man purchasing a complicated knife — and a headache. Tracks braid, unbraid, and lie with professional cheer. Quill files Lowhedge under “paid in blood or nearly” and takes a pull.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Road Hygiene
frames.body: East wind brings sugar lies and iron honesty in the same breath. A road-vermin kid offers directions priced in half-truths. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: Clouds drag shadows across cane-colored grass like slow warrants. Road vermin sell water and buy stories about numbered men who would not quit. Lyra’s name leaves your mouth without shaking — progress, of a kind.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: A collar ring half-buried in mud still remembers a neck. Somewhere east, ink still invents people into inventory. Ash sets the pace like a man owed a name and too stubborn to renegotiate.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
frames.body: The road offers a fork that is really a dare. Ash rolls a shoulder that still remembers iron’s manners. Continue — soft roads make soft collars again.

frames.title: Quill's Lesson
frames.body: “Steel answers collar-law better than ink,” Quill says, wiping ichor from the bolt like a clerk correcting a sum that insulted him. He drills stance, breath, and the difference between killing for a warrant and killing for rage — then takes a pull and adds, “Rage is fine. Just don’t let it hold the damn map. And don’t quit mid-swing.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
frames.body: Your shadow arrives first and looks less free than you feel. Tonight’s camp will argue theology with mosquitoes and memory. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

frames.title: Warrant Grammar
frames.body: Quill makes you read a blank warrant aloud until the clauses stop sounding like mercy — alive-if-possible, dead-if-necessary, property-of-no-man once the seal dries. “Paper is a leash that points both ways,” he drawls. “Hold your end. Don’t let Cade’s clerks invent the other.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ash Aside
frames.body: Road dust writes temporary freemarks on every boot. Quill’s crossbow speaks better law than half the magistrates east — and swears better too. Quill nods toward the darker horizon without ceremony.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
frames.body: A creek argues with stones like a tired magistrate on half pay. You practice stance until rage becomes usable instead of loud. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

frames.title: Collar-Yard Rumor
frames.body: A peddler swears Candlemire hangs rings by size, not by name — and that song-thralls get loft windows so buyers can hear before they bid. Quill does not drink to that. He drinks past it, scarred eye fixed east, and says only, “Then we learn loft latches.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Powder Prayer
frames.body: Marshal Halbrecht Quill lights a match off a warrant nail, drinks, and looks almost human. Quill drawls, “Paper freedom first — real freedom files later — don’t quit in between.” Paper freedom first; real freedom files later — Quill’s oldest sermon.

frames.title: Campfire Question
frames.body: Night fire snaps. Quill boils bitter tea that tastes like boiled warrants, spikes it from the flask, and asks what you will do if Lyra is already broken past rescue. The question sits between you like a third traveler with muddy boots and no manners. His scarred eye does not blink.

choices.label: I bring her home anyway
choices.approach: Refuse the premise that anyone is past saving.

choices.label: Then Candlemire pays in blood
choices.approach: If she is gone, Cade Mire still owns the debt.

choices.label: I will decide when I see her
choices.approach: Keep counsel until the yard is real.

frames.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Ash learns that freemen still bleed; they just choose the reason and refuse the exit. You walk west, then south, then wherever vengeance keeps maps — stubborn as weather.

frames.title: Warrant One
frames.body: A farmstead burned for unpaid tribute; the killer rides with a brand that matches Cade’s outer seals like a signature on arson. Quill’s warrant names him Vern of Lowhedge. “Alive if possible,” Quill says. “Dead if the world insists on being itself.”

frames.body: Ash roofs. A child’s doll face-down in soot. Tracks lead into scrub where wargs like soft meat and softer excuses. Somewhere ahead, Vern laughs at a joke only hunters and monsters find funny.

frames.title: Vern Cornered
frames.body: Vern sits against a tree with a leg broken by his own horse — comedy the forest did not request. He begs for Cade’s protection and offers a torn ledger page with women’s names. Lyra’s sits three lines down, sold as “song-thrall, unbroken.” Vern smiles like that should buy him sunrise.

choices.label: Bind him for trial
choices.approach: Alive for Quill’s warrant and a clean conscience.

choices.label: End him for Lowhedge
choices.approach: Justice for ash roofs; take the page either way.

choices.label: Press for Candlemire routes
choices.approach: Pain or promise — get the eastern road correct.

frames.title: Name on Paper
frames.body: You say Lyra aloud until the vowels stop shaking and start sounding like a plan. Quill packs Vern’s routes — or what remains of them — and points toward a freeman town that stamps men as unpaid labor instead of numbered iron. Dawn smells like pine, powder, and unfinished debts.

frames.body: Chain-Road mist sits on the world like damp wool and worse math. The Wilderland watches and declines to explain itself. Ash sets the pace like a man owed a name and too stubborn to renegotiate.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Yard Gossip
frames.body: A roadside shrine wears three names and one empty nail. A thrall brand on a fence rail dares you to look away. The road keeps its receipts in blood and dust alike.
